title: More colors in status column
excerpt: We use status columns for alot of things, and a few times now I have run out of colors to choose from.  I do not necessarily want to switch this to a dropdown column, as that can be a bit too unwieldy for my users.  Just…

posts.author: travis.mcclellan (Travis McClellan)
posts.cookedHtml: We use status columns for alot of things, and a few times now I have run out of colors to choose from. I do not necessarily want to switch this to a dropdown column, as that can be a bit too unwieldy for my users. Just curious why the color palette stopped at 40 and not more?

posts.author: andrewalmand (Andrew Almand)
posts.cookedHtml:
@travis.mcclellan

The color pallet was actually extended to 40 sometime last year I think. I would imagine its in the works to add more, although I know nothing of the requirements or limitations that comes with it.

@TRB-monday.com Might be able to shed light on if this is in the works or not.

posts.author: ajwhitman (AJ W)
posts.cookedHtml:
The option to add custom colors (using hex codes or something) would also make this more customizable!

I’m also curious, though, why the colors are listed in a seemingly random order? In some cases I want to use a gradient, or pick a specific shade, and it’s hard to search for “just the right blue” when they’re all over the place.

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @travis.mcclellan, @andrewalmand, and @ajwhitman!

Like Andrew mentioned, we did extend the labels to 40 earlier this year. I’m checking with our team to see if more are in the works and will let you know!

Another option depending on your team’s preferences could also be the tags column, which is visual/colorful like the status column: The Tags Column.

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey all! Just wanted to follow up on this: we aren’t currently planning to add more labels (it’s not on our roadmap right now), but I’ve passed your feedback to the team and will let you know if I get any updates!

@travis.mcclellan @andrewalmand @ajwhitman

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
For our purposes, there a MORE than enough colors. However, it would be great if colors could be assigned to more that one label.

@TRB-monday.com

posts.author: andrewalmand (Andrew Almand)
posts.cookedHtml:
@JCorrell

Good point! Likewise it’s not the color options that limit us, but the number of labels being tied to the colors. Since that number limits automation options, colors being assigned to more than 1 label would solve that for me at least.

posts.author: LaurentConroux (Laurent Conroux)
posts.cookedHtml:
Absolutely.
For example lets say I have a status column with the result of a deal offer.
There are several statuses that might mean the deal failed or has been closed.
The client would like to have the same color for all the “won/closed” statuses, and another color for all the “lost/no go” ones.
Today we would have to add a specific status column and automation rules to get that data on the groups and boards summary rows.

posts.author: TaylorTedesco (Taylor Tedesco)
posts.cookedHtml: Yep I definitely need this. Right now I just use two very similar colors but I am not a fan.

posts.author: jtbs (Theo S)
posts.cookedHtml: seconding (thirding?) this – it’s frustrating having the amount of sorting I can do being arbitrarily capped at 40 items just because colors are non-reusable.

posts.author: alexcamp (Alexandre Campagna)
posts.cookedHtml:
Instead of adding more colors, why not make a HEX color picker for us to choose

?

posts.author: JNewman2020 (Jennifer)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed and I want the ability to use the same color for multiple statuses. It should be up to us if that makes sense for our workflow. When you have a status that has multiple options, it can look like crap. I want to be able to bunch similar statuses together (Paid Social, Org Social)

posts.author: Carol_Hiscock (Carol Hiscock)
posts.cookedHtml:
Would add that adding customizable colors, to be able to use our branded pallette would make this tool much more appealing to our leadership team and sharing with clients.

Please reconsider adding the feature to choose your own colors.

posts.author: James3 (James Warner)
posts.cookedHtml: I was unaware of the colour limit! For the way we use monday at the moment this is crucial to have a lot more colours! Fingers it goes into the road map soon

posts.author: PrintGeek (Dan Neto)
posts.cookedHtml:
I agree that this feature is of much greater importance to users than it’s being given.

The limited amount of label colors is a big disappointment. Users need to create custom colours through a simple RGB/HEX code picker/input, similar to the already existing “Color Picker” column feature.

Custom colours need to be saved as a master colour and be accessible for all users and all functions so users don’t have to create the same colour across different functions within the account.

The “Red” label colour isn’t even close to red RGB red, and there only one “Red” to choose from. This isn’t acceptable and has led to confusion and productivity issue due to the lack of intuitiveness of the limited colour option that users are forced to use.

We don’t need more colours, we need control over the colours we can create ourselves. Please make this a priority.

posts.author: Wombat (Dave Hampson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Further to this, it would be to select ‘no colour’ to a given value so the cell is the same colour as the rest of the row.
We use a status column for any category where we want to restrict choice to 1 value, and having a multitude of colours makes it look very confusing without providing useful info.

posts.author: jharness (Josh Harness)
posts.cookedHtml: Rather than having a preset number of colors to select from when coloring statuses, group names, etc. Monday should either replace the pregenerated ones with an RGB/Hex code input format allowing us to pick our own colors, or integrate that alongside the color presets.

posts.author: nataliav (Natalia V)
posts.cookedHtml: Same. I am severely limited by 40 colors. @MondayDevs: Please let us have an unlimited amount of labels in the status column.

posts.author: Mel_Schulien (Mel S.)
posts.cookedHtml: Same! Would love the feature of being able to create our own colors for brand consistency.

posts.author: mindyatlongcapture (Mindy Morrison)
posts.cookedHtml: This really needs to be an option. What we use the status column for is linked to a different platform. Changing the column type to a dropdown is a huge undertaking. We have been using Monday since 2019 and it is disappointing that something as simple as adding more colors has not been implemented.

posts.author: WLane (Wally Lane)
posts.cookedHtml: Bang on! This would be amazing and would align the colour schemes to our branding colours. Voted!

posts.author: Joe_Downey (Joe Downey)
posts.cookedHtml:
Can someone chime in on the difficulty to add more colors or a color picker? It seems like a lightweight add that would be a welcome addition.

Or does someone know of a Marketplace App that would add this functionality? I like Monday, but it feels like a Frankenstein’s monster of work arounds and add-ons.

posts.author: jstokes (Joanne Stokes)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree. We need more colors (labels)! There are times I am forced to use a multi-select column because the single-select doesn’t have enough label/color options. Unfortunately, you can’t run automations when an item is created based on multi-select columns, so I’m stuck doing manual work. It’s very frustrating.

posts.author: erikhianke (Erik Hianke)
posts.cookedHtml:
It is essential that you add new colors to create new labels in the “Status” column. Interrupting certain uses of columns due to lack of resources is like suspending internal productivity which, in theory, is something simple for the team to resolve.

If you consider our opinion important, do so and we will have more positive results. Thank you Monday, I love you!

posts.author: jstokes (Joanne Stokes)
posts.cookedHtml: If the issue is color palette options, consider an alternative way to solve the problem. The real issue is we need single select columns with more than 40 options - and we probably aren’t using it as a status at that point. In all honesty, in this situation the colors probably have no connection to the selections being made. So maybe a new type of column (Single Select - Grayscale) would make more sense.

posts.author: srandall (Shannon)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is honestly a must for me.

The biggest thing for me is that I need to easily tell what status something is at a glance because I have sooo much stuff. With the current color palatte, some of the colors are so similar that the ones I need to stand out to get my attention are just blending in with the next few items.

Having a color picker would honestly be the absolute best option for everyone I think.

posts.author: Meganotto (Megan O | Monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: I also have the need for more than 40 status labels. For example we use boards for in-network payer status and also have a salesforce integration so we are limited to types of columns we can use and have well over 40 different payers.

posts.author: Bill_Stamos (Bill Stamos)
posts.cookedHtml:
Status label values should go up to allow 100 or 200 or more…
We use these for helping users pick Customer names & Project names (from a curated listing rather than having to type them). I want to use a drop-down choice and not free-text typing.

40 should not be a limit anymore with mondayDB now advertising x improved capacity, speed, etc.

monday.com

mondayDB – A brand new data architecture

mondayDB is the new data infrastructure behind our Work OS, designed to drive your best work.

posts.author: James1178 (James Pyle)
posts.cookedHtml:
I just started using Monday.com at my work and was fairly impressed until I got to the color selection. Many years of this request and all they can do is add 40 total colors, that are then randomly sorted in some weird order (as mentioned in this same thread before)

Why not just allow custom colors like every other similar piece of software does? At the very least sort the colors by the type (blues, greens, reds etc)

It’s quite mind boggling to be honest.

posts.author: cgodbou (Charles Godbout)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there !

We defenitely need an improvement here !!

Here’s my suggestion:

Let the user chooses the color of each status :

  1. Option could have no color
  2. Option could have any HEX color (Background AND Font)
  3. Option could have the same color as any other option
  4. Option considered as “Done” sould be selected by a check box beside the name of the option and not by the color box below. (To allow multiple options with the same color)

Thanks you so much !
I hope this improvment will be prioritized!

Cheers!

posts.author: cire12 (Eric Powelson)
posts.cookedHtml:
I just posted a question about this very topic before finding this thread. I was fairly convinced that I must be doing something wrong because this implementation is so bad. From forcing you to apply a color to EVERYTHING (some of my board look TERRIBLE because of this), to not being able to use the same color for more than one item, to not being able to have a simple color picker (in addition to the default color palette). This is all table stakes stuff. And the fact that this has been a thread that goes back to 2021? Unacceptable.

I have stakeholders that want to leave the platform over this, which is wild because monday.com is SOLID in so many other areas. Please fix this.

posts.author: dennisndean (Dennis Dean)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding another +1 to this feature request. I would like the ability to create custom colors at the company or Workspace level and have them be available to all users.

posts.author: stephanie.moore (Stephanie Moore)
posts.cookedHtml:
Bill Stamos:

40 should not be a limit anymore with mondayDB now advertising x improved capacity, speed, etc.

I agree with Bill — the status column shouldn’t be limited to 40 options. We use this field across all our practices, and the limit poses a problem as we continue to grow. Status columns also work better in visualization widgets, which is why switching to a Dropdown isn’t ideal.

posts.author: cgodbou (Charles Godbout)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

Any updates regarding this feature request? Is it on the road map?

Please prioritize this one.

Thanks

posts.author: ACD (Alexandre Derycz)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding my voice here as well - more colors and the ability to use the same color would be a lifesaver!
